methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Vyriy Drone is a Growth-stage company in Defense & Security founded by Oleksiy Babenko, with $4M+ in total capital raised via M&A as of 2025. The broader Defense & Security sector encompasses 24 peer companies, including 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers such as Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

Given the early-stage maturity and limited availability of same-stage comparable data within the narrow peer set, only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis anchors to the $4M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $5M. Peer-median valuation was not applicable; while 24 same-sector peers exist, insufficient granular comparables at matching stage and valuation tier precluded reliable benchmarking without introducing noise from broader sectoral medians.

The triangulated valuation range supported by available methods spans $4M to $6M, with a median point of $5M. This analysis rests primarily on stage-based progression logic rather than market-derived multiples. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue-generating comparable data points and limited visibility into near-term funding traction. Further validation would benefit from additional Growth-stage drone or autonomous systems comparables with disclosed financials.
